$comment: Single source of truth for known AI-agent config files. scan.py (detection), canonicalize.py (stub writing) and check_drift.py (stub drift guard) all derive their lists from this file so a new tool is added in exactly one place. Node reads it too where it needs the config-file list. scan_patterns are the richer detection globs; stub_paths/stub_content mirror the byte-exact stubs canonicalize.py writes today. canonicalizable:false means a scan-only tool that is intentionally not migrated (see roo).

tools.label: Claude Code
tools.stub_content:
@AGENTS.md
<!-- Canonical instructions live in AGENTS.md. Keep this file as an import stub only. -->

tools.label: Cursor
tools.stub_content: All agent instructions live in AGENTS.md (single source of truth). Do not add rules here.

tools.label: Windsurf
tools.stub_content: All agent instructions live in AGENTS.md (single source of truth). Do not add rules here.

# GitHub Copilot instructions

Canonical agent instructions live in `AGENTS.md`. Keep this stub minimal; do not duplicate rules here.

tools.label: Gemini CLI
tools.stub_content:
# Gemini instructions

Canonical agent instructions live in `AGENTS.md`. Prefer configuring Gemini CLI `contextFileName` to `AGENTS.md`; keep this file as a pointer only.

tools.label: Cline
tools.stub_content: Canonical agent instructions live in AGENTS.md. Keep this Cline pointer minimal and do not duplicate rules here.

tools.label: Roo
tools.canonicalizable_note: Roo is a rules-directory tool (.roo/rules/*.md) with no single conventional stub-file location (no flat .roorules is scanned). Collapsing a rules directory into one canonical pointer requires the same bespoke directory-handling Cursor has, which is semantic-merge logic that belongs in SKILL.md and is out of scope for this refactor. Marked canonicalizable:false so it is an explicit, documented scan-only opt-out rather than a silent drop; the consistency test enforces this.

tools.label: Continue
tools.stub_content: All agent instructions live in AGENTS.md (single source of truth). Do not add rules here.

tools.label: Trae
tools.canonicalizable_note: Trae's project rules live at .trae/rules/project_rules.md (Markdown, with an optional user_rules.md for personal/global preferences that is intentionally not scanned as a repo config file) with no flat single-file legacy location to stub, the same shape as Roo. Marked canonicalizable:false so it is an explicit, documented scan-only opt-out rather than a silent drop; the consistency test enforces this.
